result.output:
Explanation: The study "Nonacosan-10-ol and n-Alkanes in Leaves of Pinus pinaster" (2020) analyzes the cuticular wax of Pinus pinaster, a Mediterranean species in the genus Pinus, which is found in the Northern Hemisphere and temperate latitudes. According to the abstract, the content of nonacosan-10-ol in the leaf cuticular wax is high (77.1% on average), making it the most dominant compound. This matches the context of the question regarding the dominant compound in the first line of defense (cuticular wax) as discussed in the "Results and Discussion" section of the referenced study [28183].
Exact Answer: nonacosan-10-ol
Confidence: 100%
